title: மின்மினி — Minminni (Fireflies) — EP02 Short
moral: You can't own something wild. True beauty is in freedom.

kaviya.name: Kaviya
kaviya.description: curious, empathetic Tamil girl — emotional arc is KEY

paati.name: Paati
paati.dialogue_type: none — humming / sighs / gentle laughs only

scenes.speaker: Arjun
scenes.emotion_description: Pure boyish excitement. Calling friends with infectious energy.
scenes.tts_prompt: Arjun is bursting with energy, inviting his friends to play. High-pitched, enthusiastic, fast delivery. A 7-year-old boy's unbridled joy.
scenes.visual_note: Arjun running barefoot through courtyard at dusk, arms wide, calling out.

scenes.speaker: Arjun
scenes.emotion_description: Sudden shift from playful to awestruck. Discovery moment.
scenes.tts_prompt: Arjun spots the fireflies and his energy transforms from playful to genuinely awestruck. Breathy, slower, drawn-out 'Ayo!' and 'Paaru!' like he can't believe what he's seeing.
scenes.visual_note: First fireflies appear. Arjun's eyes huge, pointing off-screen.

scenes.speaker: Kaviya
scenes.english_gloss: Catch them! I'm coming too! Run, run!
scenes.emotion_description: Kaviya joins the excitement, taking charge. Energetic and commanding.
scenes.tts_prompt: Kaviya is excited but also taking charge. Her voice is confident, playful, a bit commanding. She's rallying the others to join her. Clear, energetic delivery with youthful glee.
scenes.visual_note: All three kids running through yard chasing fireflies, arms outstretched, laughing.

scenes.speaker: Arjun
scenes.tamil_dialogue: Pudinga, pudinga! Bottle-la podunga! Paaru, evvalavu iruku!
scenes.english_gloss: Wait, wait! Put them in a bottle! Look how many there are!
scenes.emotion_description: Arjun has an idea. Excited but a bit scheming. Not yet aware of the consequence.
scenes.tts_prompt: Arjun has just thought of something clever. His voice is excited, conspiratorial, like he's about to do something he's proud of. A hint of playful mischief, not malice.
scenes.visual_note: Arjun holds up a clear glass jar with 8-10 fireflies glowing inside. Kids' faces lit by jar glow.

scenes.speaker: Kaviya
scenes.tamil_dialogue: Arjun... paaru. Bottle-la potathuku apram, minminni poochiyoda velicham konjam, konjama koraiyithu!
scenes.english_gloss: Arjun... look. Since we put them in the bottle, the fireflies' light is getting dimmer and dimmer.
scenes.emotion_description: Realization dawns. Kaviya notices something is wrong. Gentle worry, not panic.
scenes.tts_prompt: Kaviya is making a careful observation. Her voice is softer now, thoughtful, a bit sad. She's noticed the fireflies' light dimming and it's bothering her. Speak slowly, with a hint of sadness and concern, like she's putting pieces together.
scenes.visual_note: Close-up of jar. Firefly glow noticeably dimmer than scene 4. Kaviya holds it, looking worried.

scenes.speaker: Kaviya
scenes.tamil_dialogue: Antha poochigaluku bottle-ulla adaipattu irukrathu pidikala. Velila vitralama?
scenes.english_gloss: Those little lights don't like being trapped in the bottle. Should we let them go?
scenes.emotion_description: Kaviya proposes release. Gentle, hopeful, asking for agreement. The turning point.
scenes.tts_prompt: Kaviya is speaking from her heart now. Her voice is hopeful, kind, and a little questioning (she's asking Arjun). Speak softly but with quiet confidence. There's compassion and wisdom in this line.
scenes.visual_note: Kaviya holds jar to her chest, looking at Arjun with earnest, pleading eyes. Arjun's mischief melts into understanding. He nods.

scenes.speaker: Kaviya
scenes.emotion_description: Kaviya releases the fireflies. Quiet, gentle, almost like a blessing.
scenes.tts_prompt: Kaviya is releasing the fireflies like she's giving them a blessing. Whisper-soft delivery, almost reverent. Slow, deliberate, each word separate. Like she's whispering to the fireflies themselves.
scenes.visual_note: Slow motion. Jar tips, fireflies spiral upward into dark sky. Yard lights up dramatically.

scenes.speaker: Meenu
scenes.tamil_dialogue: Paaru, Kaviya akka... romba azhagaa irukku.
scenes.english_gloss: Look, Kaviya sister... it's so beautiful.
scenes.emotion_description: Meenu speaks for the first time. Pure, innocent wonder. The culmination.
scenes.tts_prompt: Meenu is awestruck. Her voice is small, pure, and genuinely amazed. She's looking up at hundreds of fireflies and finding the words. Slightly breathy, full of wonder. The youngest voice in the film, full of innocence.
scenes.visual_note: Wide shot of entire yard filled with fireflies. Three kids stand together, looking up. Paati smiles from porch in background. Peak beauty frame.

Paati.character_arc: silent observer → warm smile. NO DIALOGUE. Humming/sighs/gentle laugh only.

optimizations.stability_adaptive: lower stability (0.3–0.4) for emotional variation, higher (0.5) for clarity

script_qa_checklist.tamil_authenticity: colloquial, diaspora-appropriate, NOT formal literary Tamil
script_qa_checklist.emotional_arc: excitement → wonder → joy → mischief → realization → compassion → reverence → awe
script_qa_checklist.character_consistency: Arjun = energetic boy, Kaviya = thoughtful/empathetic, Meenu = shy but sweet, Paati = warm observer
script_qa_checklist.tts_readiness: each scene has emotion descriptor + tts_prompt for Claude to generate voice instructions
script_qa_checklist.visual_sync: each dialogue line has visual_note for animator to match lip-sync

notes_for_voice_testing: Test Bradford (Arjun) in scenes 1–2. Does he capture boyish wonder AND excitement?
Test Arabella (Kaviya) across 5–7. CRITICAL: Does she show emotional range from joy → sadness → hope → awe?
Test Hope (Meenu) in scene 8. Is her voice small, innocent, full of amazement?
Paati scenes: Only use Monika Sogam for humming/sighs. NO VOICE DIALOGUE.
ElevenLabs v3 style parameter: test 'excited' vs. 'gentle' in samples before full pipeline run.
